title: bidens pilosa subvar. radiata
intro: Bidens pilosa subvar. radiata, also known as Creeping Beggarweed, is a type of flowering plant native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues. Bidens pilosa subvar. radiata is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
